I am planning a new `./hsim` feature set that exposes M3..M8 (icount, BBV, slice, archive, replay) as first-class subcommands. They are currently only reachable via wrappers in `tests/demos/demo_*.sh`. I need a precise map of:

1. The current `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hsim` script structure -- list every subparser, the helper functions (`_resolve_artifacts`, `_DEFAULT_JOBS`, etc.), and the argv split-on-`--` trick the F8 `demo` command uses. Quote the line ranges, do not paste large blocks.

2. The `/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/tools/orchestrator/` Python module CLI -- specifically `orchestrator.main`, `orchestrator.checkpoint.write_archive`, and the `run` + `replay-checkpoint` subcommands. List the entrypoint files and the public function signatures (no need to read line-by-line, just enough so I can wrap them without re-implementing).

3. What `QEMU+plugin` invocation lines the five demo scripts share (`-machine andes_ae350`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`, `-nographic`, `-semihosting-config`, `-plugin <PLUGIN>,outfile=...`). Confirm whether `tests/lib/paths.sh` already centralises this and whether any existing helper in `tools/orchestrator/` already builds the argv.

4. Whether there is any existing single-shot CLI (e.g. `python -m orchestrator.profile` or similar) that already runs an M4 profile pass, an M6 archive emit, or an M7 replay -- so I can wrap that instead of re-implementing the QEMU/plugin argv.

5. The `tests/fixtures/icount_spin.elf` -- where is it built, and is the CSR-less fixture set documented anywhere (Makefile target list)?

Report back with file paths, function names, and line numbers. Under 500 words. I am building a plan; I will not execute changes from your output.

Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Triggered explicitly via /spec-human <topic>, or when the user says "write a human spec", "create a review-ready design doc", "spec this out for review", or "I need a spec for X before we build it". Produces an ASCII markdown document at ./specs/<slug>.md aimed at peer engineers and tech leads who need to validate core architecture and trade-offs in 5 minutes. Do NOT auto-trigger on general design questions or coding tasks - this is a deliberate review gate that only fires on explicit request. For LLM-targeted specs (future sibling skill), use /spec-llm instead.

Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,

Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.

Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""hsim - end-user entry point for the Andes hybrid simulator.
3	
4	Single-file driver. Wraps the developer-grade scripts (setup.sh,
5	tests/e2e.sh, scripts/run_e2e.sh) behind four subcommands:
6	
7	    ./hsim build [--what TARGET]
8	    ./hsim run <elf-path> --mode csr|pc [--verbose]
9	    ./hsim test [--filter REGEX] [--mode csr|pc|both]
10	    ./hsim list <fixtures|demos|modes>
11	    ./hsim doctor
12	    ./hsim demo <T1..T7|short-name> [-- KEY=VAL ...]
13	
14	Python 3 stdlib only; no pip deps.
15	"""
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import argparse
19	import os
20	import re
21	import shlex
22	import shutil
23	import subprocess
24	import sys
25	import tempfile
26	import time
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	
30	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
31	
32	
33	# --- F1: list ---------------------------------------------------------------
34	
35	# M1..M8 short table. Authoritative source: docs/USER_GUIDE.md sections 3-4.
36	# Kept here so `./hsim list modes` does not need to grep USER_GUIDE at runtime.
37	MODES = [
38	    ("M1", "csr",           "csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1} insn match (default)"),
39	    ("M2", "pc",            "ELF symbols _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"),
40	    ("M3", "icount",        "retired-insn count trigger"),
41	    ("M4", "bbv",           "basic-block vector profiling"),
42	    ("M5", "slice",         "slice-consume; vsim resumes from a captured slice"),
43	    ("M6", "archive",       "checkpoint archive replay"),
44	    ("M7", "replay",        "deterministic re-execution of a captured run"),
45	    ("M8", "end-of-program", "natural program exit terminates the round-trip"),
46	]
47	
48	
49	def parse_fixtures(makefile: Path) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
50	    """Extract fixture names + family from tests/fixtures/Makefile.
51	
52	    Returns rows (name, family) in stable, family-grouped order. Family is
53	    the variable they came from (RT_FIXTURES, RT_C_FIXTURES, ...).
54	    """
55	    text = makefile.read_text()
56	    rows: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
57	    # Variable definitions look like:
58	    #   RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases \
59	    #                  rt_long_roi
60	    # We capture everything up to a non-continuation newline.
61	    var_pattern = re.compile(
62	        r"^(?P<var>[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\s*:?=\s*(?P<body>(?:[^\n]*\\\n)*[^\n]*)$",
63	        re.MULTILINE,
64	    )
65	    interesting = {
66	        "RT_FIXTURES":      "asm",
67	        "RT_C_FIXTURES":    "c",
68	        "RT_C_V_FIXTURES":  "c+rvv",
69	        "PROBE_V_FIXTURES": "probe",
70	    }
71	    seen: set[str] = set()
72	    for m in var_pattern.finditer(text):
73	        var = m.group("var")
74	        if var not in interesting:
75	            continue
76	        family = interesting[var]
77	        body = m.group("body").replace("\\\n", " ")
78	        for name in body.split():
79	            if name and name not in seen:
80	                rows.append((name, family))
81	                seen.add(name)
82	    # Pick up the legacy hand-listed ELF targets.
83	    for name in ("handoff_roundtrip",):
84	        if name not in seen:
85	            rows.append((name, "asm"))
86	            seen.add(name)
87	    return rows
88	
89	
90	def list_fixtures() -> int:
91	    makefile = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "Makefile"
92	    if not makefile.is_file():
93	        print(f"hsim: missing {makefile}", file=sys.stderr)
94	        return 2
95	    rows = parse_fixtures(makefile)
96	    width = max(len(name) for name, _ in rows)
97	    print(f"{'FIXTURE'.ljust(width)}  FAMILY")
98	    for name, family in rows:
99	        print(f"{name.ljust(width)}  {family}")
100	    return 0
101	
102	
103	def list_demos() -> int:
104	    demo_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "demos"
105	    if not demo_dir.is_dir():
106	        print(f"hsim: missing {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
107	        return 2
108	    names = sorted(p.stem for p in demo_dir.glob("demo_*.sh"))
109	    if not names:
110	        print(f"hsim: no demos found under {demo_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
111	        return 2
112	    for name in names:
113	        print(name)
114	    return 0
115	
116	
117	def list_modes() -> int:
118	    width_id = max(len(m[0]) for m in MODES)
119	    width_key = max(len(m[1]) for m in MODES)
120	    print(f"{'ID'.ljust(width_id)}  {'KEY'.ljust(width_key)}  DESCRIPTION")
121	    for mode_id, key, desc in MODES:
122	        print(f"{mode_id.ljust(width_id)}  {key.ljust(width_key)}  {desc}")
123	    return 0
124	
125	
126	def cmd_list(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
127	    return {
128	        "fixtures": list_fixtures,
129	        "demos":    list_demos,
130	        "modes":    list_modes,
131	    }[args.target]()
132	
133	
134	# --- F2: build --------------------------------------------------------------
135	
136	# Each target maps to a list of subprocess argv lists. We delegate to the
137	# existing scripts (setup.sh, scripts/build_*.sh, Makefiles) -- no logic
138	# duplicated here.
139	def _build_steps(target: str) -> list[list[str]]:
140	    qemu     = ["bash", "scripts/build_qemu.sh"]
141	    vsim     = ["bash", "scripts/build_vsim.sh"]
142	    plugin   = ["make", "-C", "qemu_plugin", "QEMU_ROOT=./QEMU"]
143	    simpoint = ["bash", "scripts/build_simpoint.sh"]
144	    fixtures = ["make", "-C", "tests/fixtures"]
145	    setup    = ["bash", "setup.sh"]
146	    return {
147	        "all":      [setup, fixtures],
148	        "qemu":     [qemu],
149	        "vsim":     [vsim],
150	        "plugin":   [plugin],
151	        "simpoint": [simpoint],
152	        "fixtures": [fixtures],
153	    }[target]
154	
155	
156	def cmd_build(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
157	    try:
158	        steps = _build_steps(args.what)
159	    except KeyError:
160	        print(f"hsim: unknown build target: {args.what}", file=sys.stderr)
161	        return 2
162	    for argv in steps:
163	        line = shlex.join(argv)
164	        if args.dry_run:
165	            print(line)
166	            continue
167	        print(f"[hsim] {line}")
168	        rc = subprocess.run(argv, cwd=REPO_ROOT).returncode
169	        if rc != 0:
170	            print(f"hsim: build step failed (rc={rc}): {line}", file=sys.stderr)
171	            return rc
172	    return 0
173	
174	
175	# --- F3: run ----------------------------------------------------------------
176	
177	# Default build-artifact locations. Honored as env-var overrides so the
178	# F3 RED test can simulate a missing vsim binary without disturbing the
179	# real one.
180	_DEFAULT_ARTIFACTS = {
181	    "vsim":   REPO_ROOT / "verilator" / "build" / "sim_ax45mpv_premium",
182	    "qemu":   REPO_ROOT / "build" / "qemu" / "qemu-system-riscv64",
183	    "plugin": REPO_ROOT / "qemu_plugin" / "libhybrid_handoff.so",
184	}
185	_ARTIFACT_ENV = {"vsim": "VSIM_BIN", "qemu": "VSIM_QEMU", "plugin": "VSIM_PLUGIN"}
186	
187	
188	def _resolve_artifacts() -> dict[str, Path]:
189	    return {
190	        label: Path(os.environ.get(_ARTIFACT_ENV[label]) or default)
191	        for label, default in _DEFAULT_ARTIFACTS.items()
192	    }
193	
194	
195	def cmd_run(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
196	    elf = Path(args.elf)
197	    if not elf.is_absolute():
198	        elf = (Path.cwd() / elf).resolve()
199	    if not elf.is_file():
200	        print(f"hsim: ELF not found: {args.elf}", file=sys.stderr)
201	        return 2
202	
203	    artifacts = _resolve_artifacts()
204	    for label, path in artifacts.items():
205	        if not path.is_file():
206	            print(f"hsim: {label} not built -- run ./hsim build", file=sys.stderr)
207	            return 2
208	
209	    env = os.environ.copy()
210	    env["FIXTURE"]      = elf.stem
211	    env["TRIGGER_MODE"] = args.mode
212	    env["VSIM_ELF"]     = str(elf)
213	    env["VSIM_BIN"]     = str(artifacts["vsim"])
214	    env["VSIM_QEMU"]    = str(artifacts["qemu"])
215	    env["VSIM_PLUGIN"]  = str(artifacts["plugin"])
216	
217	    cmd = ["bash", str(REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "e2e.sh")]
218	    if args.verbose:
219	        print(f"[hsim] FIXTURE={env['FIXTURE']} TRIGGER_MODE={env['TRIGGER_MODE']} "
220	              f"VSIM_ELF={env['VSIM_ELF']}", flush=True)
221	        print(f"[hsim] exec: {shlex.join(cmd)}", flush=True)
222	    return subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=REPO_ROOT, env=env).returncode
223	
224	
225	# --- F4: test ---------------------------------------------------------------
226	
227	# Default JOBS cap. The 128-core dev host saturates if scripts/run_e2e.sh's
228	# default (~nproc) is used, so we pin to 8. --jobs N overrides.
229	_DEFAULT_JOBS = 8
230	
231	
232	def _summarize_results(log_dir: Path) -> tuple[int, int, int, list[Path]]:
233	    results = log_dir / "results.tsv"
234	    p = f = s = 0
235	    failed_logs: list[Path] = []
236	    if not results.is_file():
237	        return p, f, s, failed_logs
238	    for line in results.read_text().splitlines():
239	        parts = line.split("\t")
240	        if len(parts) < 3:
241	            continue
242	        mode, name, status = parts[0], parts[1], parts[2]
243	        if status == "PASS":
244	            p += 1
245	        elif status == "FAIL":
246	            f += 1
247	            failed_logs.append(log_dir / f"{mode}--{name}.log")
248	        elif status == "SKIP":
249	            s += 1
250	    return p, f, s, failed_logs
251	
252	
253	def cmd_test(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
254	    artifacts = _resolve_artifacts()
255	    log_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="hsim-test-"))
256	
257	    env = os.environ.copy()
258	    env["JOBS"]    = str(args.jobs)
259	    env["MODE"]    = args.mode
260	    env["LOG_DIR"] = str(log_dir)
261	    env["FILTER"]  = args.filter or ""
262	    env["VSIM_BIN"]    = str(artifacts["vsim"])
263	    env["VSIM_QEMU"]   = str(artifacts["qemu"])
264	    env["VSIM_PLUGIN"] = str(artifacts["plugin"])
265	
266	    cmd = ["bash", str(REPO_ROOT / "scripts" / "run_e2e.sh")]
267	    if args.dry_run:
268	        print(f"JOBS={env['JOBS']}")
269	        print(f"MODE={env['MODE']}")
270	        print(f"FILTER={env['FILTER']}")
271	        print(f"LOG_DIR={env['LOG_DIR']}")
272	        print(f"exec: {shlex.join(cmd)}")
273	        return 0
274	
275	    start = time.monotonic()
276	    rc = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=REPO_ROOT, env=env).returncode
277	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
278	
279	    p, f, s, failed_logs = _summarize_results(log_dir)
280	    print(f"PASS {p}, FAIL {f}, SKIP {s} (elapsed: {elapsed:.1f}s)")
281	    if failed_logs:
282	        print("Failed test logs:")
283	        for fp in failed_logs:
284	            print(f"  {fp}")
285	    return rc
286	
287	
288	# --- F7: doctor (env-health diagnoser) --------------------------------------
289	
290	# Each check returns rows of (name, status, detail). Statuses:
291	#   PASS - the resource exists and is usable.
292	#   FAIL - missing/broken; exit code 1 if any present.
293	#   WARN - degraded but does not block build/test.
294	#   INFO - informational (host facts), never affects exit code.
295	_DoctorRow = tuple[str, str, str]
296	
297	
298	def _read_hybrid_toolchain() -> str | None:
299	    """Locate HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN: env first, then config.env fallback."""
300	    tc = os.environ.get("HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN")
301	    if tc:
302	        return tc
303	    cfg = REPO_ROOT / "config.env"
304	    if not cfg.is_file():
305	        return None
306	    pattern = re.compile(r"^\s*(?:export\s+)?HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN\s*=\s*(.+?)\s*$")
307	    for line in cfg.read_text().splitlines():
308	        m = pattern.match(line)
309	        if m:
310	            return m.group(1).strip().strip('"').strip("'")
311	    return None
312	
313	
314	def _doctor_artifacts() -> list[_DoctorRow]:
315	    rows: list[_DoctorRow] = []
316	    for label, path in _resolve_artifacts().items():
317	        if path.is_file():
318	            rows.append((label, "PASS", str(path)))
319	        else:
320	            rows.append((label, "FAIL",
321	                         f"missing {path} -- run ./hsim build"))
322	    simpoint = REPO_ROOT / "tools" / "simpoint" / "bin" / "simpoint"
323	    if simpoint.is_file():
324	        rows.append(("simpoint", "PASS", str(simpoint)))
325	    else:
326	        rows.append(("simpoint", "FAIL",
327	                     f"missing {simpoint} -- run ./hsim build --what simpoint"))
328	    fix_dir = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures"
329	    elfs = sorted(fix_dir.glob("*.elf")) if fix_dir.is_dir() else []
330	    if elfs:
331	        rows.append(("fixtures", "PASS", f"{len(elfs)} ELFs in {fix_dir}"))
332	    else:
333	        rows.append(("fixtures", "FAIL",
334	                     f"no *.elf in {fix_dir} -- run make -C tests/fixtures"))
335	    return rows
336	
337	
338	def _doctor_submodules() -> list[_DoctorRow]:
339	    markers = [
340	        ("QEMU/",           REPO_ROOT / "QEMU" / "configure"),
341	        ("verilator/",      REPO_ROOT / "verilator" / "CMakeLists.txt"),
342	        ("tools/simpoint/", REPO_ROOT / "tools" / "simpoint" / "Makefile"),
343	    ]
344	    rows: list[_DoctorRow] = []
345	    for name, marker in markers:
346	        if marker.is_file():
347	            rows.append((name, "PASS", str(marker.relative_to(REPO_ROOT))))
348	        else:
349	            rows.append((name, "FAIL",
350	                         f"missing {marker} -- "
351	                         f"run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'"))
352	    return rows
353	
354	
355	def _doctor_toolchain() -> list[_DoctorRow]:
356	    rows: list[_DoctorRow] = []
357	    for tool in ("ninja", "make", "podman"):
358	        found = shutil.which(tool)
359	        if found:
360	            rows.append((tool, "PASS", found))
361	        else:
362	            severity = "WARN" if tool == "podman" else "FAIL"
363	            hint = {
364	                "ninja":  "needed by ./hsim build of QEMU",
365	                "make":   "needed by ./hsim build of fixtures + simpoint",
366	                "podman": "needed by ./hsim build of vsim (or docker shim)",
367	            }[tool]
368	            rows.append((tool, severity, f"not on PATH -- {hint}"))
369	    tc = _read_hybrid_toolchain()
370	    if not tc:
371	        rows.append(("HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN", "WARN",
372	                     "not set -- needed for fixture build "
373	                     "(see config.env)"))
374	    else:
375	        gcc = Path(tc) / "bin" / "riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc"
376	        if gcc.is_file():
377	            rows.append(("riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc", "PASS", str(gcc)))
378	        else:
379	            rows.append(("riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc", "FAIL",
380	                         f"missing {gcc} -- check HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN={tc}"))
381	    return rows
382	
383	
384	def _doctor_runtime() -> list[_DoctorRow]:
385	    rows: list[_DoctorRow] = []
386	    nproc = os.cpu_count() or 1
387	    rows.append(("nproc", "INFO", str(nproc)))
388	    shm = Path("/dev/shm")
389	    if shm.is_dir():
390	        free_gb = shutil.disk_usage(shm).free // (1024 ** 3)
391	        status = "PASS" if free_gb >= 4 else "WARN"
392	        rows.append(("/dev/shm free", status, f"{free_gb} GB"))
393	    else:
394	        rows.append(("/dev/shm", "WARN",
395	                     "not present -- e2e fixtures will fall back to $TMPDIR"))
396	    suggested = min(nproc, _DEFAULT_JOBS)
397	    rows.append(("JOBS suggestion", "INFO",
398	                 f"{suggested} (default cap = {_DEFAULT_JOBS})"))
399	    return rows
400	
401	
402	def cmd_doctor(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
403	    sections: list[tuple[str, list[_DoctorRow]]] = [
404	        ("artifacts",  _doctor_artifacts()),
405	        ("submodules", _doctor_submodules()),
406	        ("toolchain",  _doctor_toolchain()),
407	        ("runtime",    _doctor_runtime()),
408	    ]
409	    name_width = max(len(name)
410	                     for _, rows in sections
411	                     for name, _, _ in rows)
412	    totals = {"PASS": 0, "FAIL": 0, "WARN": 0, "INFO": 0}
413	    for title, rows in sections:
414	        print(f"{title}:")
415	        for name, status, detail in rows:
416	            totals[status] = totals.get(status, 0) + 1
417	            print(f"  [{status:<4}] {name.ljust(name_width)}  {detail}")
418	        print()
419	    print(f"Summary: {totals['PASS']} PASS, {totals['FAIL']} FAIL, "
420	          f"{totals['WARN']} WARN")
421	    return 1 if totals["FAIL"] > 0 else 0
422	
423	
424	# --- F8: demo ---------------------------------------------------------------
425	
426	# T1..T7 -> short-name contract. Source of truth: docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md
427	# (T-id table near the top). Filename is derived as `demo_<short>.sh` so a
428	# rename only edits one place.
429	_DEMO_ORDER = [
430	    ("T1", "csr_oneshot"),
431	    ("T2", "pc_oneshot"),
432	    ("T3", "icount_roundtrip"),
433	    ("T4", "bbv_profile"),
434	    ("T5", "slice_consume_roundtrip"),

444	def _resolve_demo(selector: str) -> tuple[str, Path] | tuple[None, None]:
445	    """Map T-id, short name, or `demo_<short>` to (short, script-path).
446	
447	    Returns (None, None) if no match. The triple form lets users copy-paste
448	    from `./hsim list demos` (which emits `demo_<short>`) too.
449	    """
450	    sel = selector.strip()
451	    for tid, short in _DEMO_ORDER:
452	        if sel == tid or sel == short or sel == f"demo_{short}":
453	            return short, _demo_script(short)
454	    return None, None
455	
456	
457	def _parse_demo_passthrough(items: list[str]) -> dict[str, str] | None:
458	    """Parse `-- KEY=VAL KEY=VAL ...` trailing args. Returns None on bad form."""
459	    env_extra: dict[str, str] = {}
460	    for it in items or []:
461	        if it == "--":
462	            continue
463	        if "=" not in it or it.startswith("="):
464	            return None
465	        k, v = it.split("=", 1)
466	        env_extra[k] = v
467	    return env_extra

470	def cmd_demo(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
471	    if not args.selector:
472	        print(
473	            "hsim: missing demo selector -- run ./hsim list demos",
474	            file=sys.stderr,
475	        )
476	        return 2

478	    short, script = _resolve_demo(args.selector)
479	    if script is None:

481	            f"hsim: unknown demo: {args.selector} -- "
482	            "run ./hsim list demos (or use T1..T7)",
483	            file=sys.stderr,
484	        )
485	        return 2
486	    if not script.is_file():
487	        print(f"hsim: demo script not found: {script}", file=sys.stderr)
488	        return 2
489	
490	    env_extra = _parse_demo_passthrough(args.extras)
491	    if env_extra is None:
492	        print(
493	            "hsim: passthrough args must be KEY=VAL form (after `--`)",
494	            file=sys.stderr,

496	        return 2

498	    artifacts = _resolve_artifacts()

504	    env = os.environ.copy()

506	    env["VSIM_QEMU"]   = str(artifacts["qemu"])

609	        description=(

646	def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:

649	        head, tail = argv[:idx], argv[idx + 1:]

652	    parser = build_parser()
653	    args = parser.parse_args(head)
654	    args.extras = tail

1	"""Orchestrator CLI entry point.
2	
3	Subcommands:
4	  dry-run <plan.yaml>          Parse the plan and print one line per planned
5	                               spawn. No process is launched.
6	  run <plan.yaml>              F4/F8: execute every step in the plan; the
7	                               final spawn's exit code is the CLI return.
8	  replay-checkpoint <snap>     F10: hydrate a snap/N archive and resume the
9	                               chosen model from it.
10	"""
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	import argparse
14	import sys
15	import tempfile
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	from orchestrator.checkpoint import replay_checkpoint
19	from orchestrator.plan import Session, load_plan
20	from orchestrator.runner import run_session
21	
22	
23	def _format_step_line(idx: int, step) -> str:
24	    line = f"  step {idx}: model={step.model} trigger={step.trigger.kind}"
25	    if step.trigger.params:
26	        params = " ".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in step.trigger.params.items())
27	        line += f" ({params})"
28	    if step.archive_as:
29	        line += f" archive_as={step.archive_as}"
30	    return line
31	
32	
33	def _print_dry_run(session: Session) -> None:
34	    print(f"session: elf={session.elf} mem_size={session.mem_size}")
35	    for idx, step in enumerate(session.steps):
36	        print(_format_step_line(idx, step))
37	
38	
39	def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
40	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="orchestrator")
41	    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)
42	
43	    dry = sub.add_parser(
44	        "dry-run", help="parse a plan YAML and print planned spawns"
45	    )
46	    dry.add_argument("plan", type=Path, help="path to a plan YAML")
47	
48	    run = sub.add_parser(
49	        "run", help="execute every step in the plan (F8 N-way loop)"
50	    )
51	    run.add_argument("plan", type=Path, help="path to a plan YAML")
52	    run.add_argument(
53	        "--project-root", type=Path, default=Path.cwd(),
54	        help="repo root (default: cwd) used to resolve ELF + artifact paths",
55	    )
56	    run.add_argument(
57	        "--workdir", type=Path, default=None,
58	        help="persist per-step state files here (default: temp dir, deleted)",
59	    )
60	    run.add_argument(
61	        "--archive-root", type=Path, default=None,
62	        help=(
63	            "resolve step.archive_as under this directory "
64	            "(default: project root)"
65	        ),
66	    )
67	
68	    replay = sub.add_parser(
69	        "replay-checkpoint",
70	        help="hydrate a snap/N archive and resume the chosen model",
71	    )
72	    replay.add_argument(
73	        "snap_dir", type=Path,
74	        help="path to a snap/N directory written by an earlier archive_as",
75	    )
76	    replay.add_argument(
77	        "--resume-with", choices=("vsim", "qemu"), default="vsim",
78	        help="model to resume the archive with (default: vsim)",
79	    )
80	    replay.add_argument(
81	        "--project-root", type=Path, default=Path.cwd(),
82	        help="repo root (default: cwd) used to resolve the archived ELF",
83	    )
84	    replay.add_argument(
85	        "--workdir", type=Path, default=None,
86	        help="persist replay state files here (default: temp dir, deleted)",
87	    )
88	
89	    return parser
90	
91	
92	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
93	    args = _build_parser().parse_args(argv)
94	    if args.cmd == "dry-run":
95	        _print_dry_run(load_plan(args.plan))
96	        return 0
97	    if args.cmd == "run":
98	        session = load_plan(args.plan)
99	        result = run_session(session, args.project_root,
100	                             work_dir=args.workdir,
101	                             archive_root=args.archive_root)
102	        return result.exit_code
103	    if args.cmd == "replay-checkpoint":
104	        if args.workdir is not None:
105	            args.workdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
106	            result = replay_checkpoint(
107	                snap_dir=args.snap_dir, model=args.resume_with,
108	                project_root=args.project_root, work_dir=args.workdir,
109	            )
110	        else:
111	            with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="orch-replay-") as tmp:
112	                result = replay_checkpoint(
113	                    snap_dir=args.snap_dir, model=args.resume_with,
114	                    project_root=args.project_root, work_dir=Path(tmp),
115	                )
116	        return result.exit_code
117	    return 2
118	
119	
120	if __name__ == "__main__":
121	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""Checkpoint archive: portable snapshot of a drained spawn.
2	
3	A snapshot directory holds three artefacts:
4	
5	  state.bin   raw hybrid_state_v1 (byte copy of the in-flight state file)
6	  mem         /dev/shm mmap contents at drain time
7	  meta.json   human-readable descriptor (schema 1; see _build_meta below)
8	
9	`mem` is large (128 MB by default), so the copy goes through
10	`cp --reflink=auto` when available - on btrfs/xfs this is a near-instant
11	COW share; on ext4 the tool transparently falls back to a sequential
12	copy. If `cp` is missing entirely (unlikely on Linux), Python's
13	`shutil.copy` takes over.
14	
15	F10 (checkpoint replay) hydrates a fresh /dev/shm mmap from `mem` and
16	spawns the chosen model with `--state-in=state.bin`. Validation rules
17	F10 enforces are encoded in `meta.json` (cpu_variant, vlen_bytes,
18	mem_size).
19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import json
23	import shutil
24	import subprocess
25	from datetime import datetime, timezone
26	from pathlib import Path
27	
28	from orchestrator.abi import (
29	    HYBRID_FLAG_FP,
30	    HYBRID_FLAG_RV64,
31	    HYBRID_FLAG_V,
32	    HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC,
33	    HYBRID_STATE_VERSION,
34	    HybridStateV1,
35	)
36	
37	_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1
38	
39	# QEMU + vsim are both built with VLEN=512 bits today (spawn.py hardcodes
40	# `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`, and the vsim CMake config picks the same
41	# CPU variant). 512 bits = 64 bytes. When a future build flips VLEN, this
42	# constant must move in lock-step with spawn.py - F10's replay validator
43	# uses it to reject archives captured under a different VLEN.
44	LIVE_VLEN_BYTES = 64
45	
46	
47	def validate_replay_meta(
48	    meta: dict,
49	    *,
50	    live_cpu_variant: str | None,
51	    live_vlen_bytes: int = LIVE_VLEN_BYTES,
52	) -> None:
53	    """Reject archive metadata that would be unsafe to replay.
54	
55	    Checks (in order, first failure wins):
56	      - schema == 1
57	      - abi_version == HYBRID_STATE_VERSION (today: 1)
58	      - abi_magic (hex string) == HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC
59	      - cpu_variant matches live binary, when BOTH sides set it; if the
60	        archive omits cpu_variant, no check (older snapshots stay valid).
61	      - vlen_bytes matches live VLEN, when archive sets it.
62	
63	    Field semantics within state.bin (GPR contents, flag bit consistency)
64	    are not this function's concern - that is enforced by
65	    `abi.validate_state_file` on the resumed drain.
66	    """
67	    schema = meta.get("schema")
68	    if schema != _SCHEMA_VERSION:
69	        raise ValueError(
70	            f"meta.schema {schema!r} != expected {_SCHEMA_VERSION}"
71	        )
72	    abi_version = meta.get("abi_version")
73	    if abi_version != HYBRID_STATE_VERSION:
74	        raise ValueError(
75	            f"meta.abi_version {abi_version!r} != expected "
76	            f"{HYBRID_STATE_VERSION}"
77	        )
78	    abi_magic_raw = meta.get("abi_magic")
79	    try:
80	        abi_magic_int = int(abi_magic_raw, 16)
81	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
82	        raise ValueError(
83	            f"meta.abi_magic {abi_magic_raw!r} is not a hex string"
84	        )
85	    if abi_magic_int != HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC:
86	        raise ValueError(
87	            f"meta.abi_magic 0x{abi_magic_int:08x} != expected "
88	            f"0x{HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC:08x}"
89	        )
90	    archive_cpu = meta.get("cpu_variant")
91	    if (archive_cpu is not None and live_cpu_variant is not None
92	            and archive_cpu != live_cpu_variant):
93	        raise ValueError(
94	            f"cpu_variant mismatch: archive={archive_cpu!r} "
95	            f"live={live_cpu_variant!r}"
96	        )
97	    archive_vlen = meta.get("vlen_bytes")
98	    if archive_vlen is not None and archive_vlen != live_vlen_bytes:
99	        raise ValueError(
100	            f"vlen_bytes mismatch: archive={archive_vlen} "
101	            f"live={live_vlen_bytes}"
102	        )
103	
104	
105	def write_archive(
106	    snap_dir: Path,
107	    *,
108	    state_path: Path,
109	    mmap_path: Path,
110	    mmap_size: int,
111	    elf_path: str,
112	    source_step_index: int,
113	    cpu_variant: str | None = None,
114	    region_id: str | None = None,
115	    slice_index: int | None = None,
116	) -> None:
117	    """Serialise (state.bin, mem, meta.json) into `snap_dir`.
118	
119	    Creates intermediate directories. Overwrites pre-existing artefacts
120	    inside `snap_dir` if any. Reads the state struct's `flags` and
121	    `vlenb` fields to populate meta.json.
122	    """
123	    snap_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
124	    shutil.copy(state_path, snap_dir / "state.bin")
125	    _reflink_or_copy(mmap_path, snap_dir / "mem")
126	    meta = _build_meta(
127	        state_path=state_path, mmap_size=mmap_size, elf_path=elf_path,
128	        source_step_index=source_step_index, cpu_variant=cpu_variant,
129	        region_id=region_id, slice_index=slice_index,
130	    )
131	    (snap_dir / "meta.json").write_text(json.dumps(meta, indent=2))
132	
133	
134	def _build_meta(
135	    *,
136	    state_path: Path,
137	    mmap_size: int,
138	    elf_path: str,
139	    source_step_index: int,
140	    cpu_variant: str | None,
141	    region_id: str | None,
142	    slice_index: int | None,
143	) -> dict:
144	    state = HybridStateV1.from_buffer_copy(state_path.read_bytes())
145	    flag_names = []
146	    if state.flags & HYBRID_FLAG_RV64:

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T3: icount switching mode (M3) end-to-end.
3	#
4	# Both QEMU1 and vsim switch via retired-instruction count alone, with
5	# no CSR markers in the workload:
6	#
7	#   QEMU1 + plugin icount=N1    -> drain at insn N1 (M3).
8	#   vsim --hybrid-icount N2     -> drain at insn N2 (M3).
9	#
10	# Workload is icount_spin.elf (CSR-less tight 2-insn RVC loop at 0x100002
11	# and 0x100004). Neither side could use csrwi 0x7C0 -- icount IS the
12	# only available drain trigger. There is no natural semihosting exit
13	# (icount_spin spins forever), so this demo asserts the round-trip
14	# DRAINS correctly at both ends; M8 is exercised by T1 / T2 / T6.
15	#
16	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
17	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
18	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
19	#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
20	#   VSIM_ELF      CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
21	#   QEMU_ICOUNT   plugin icount drain target (default: 10000)
22	#   VSIM_ICOUNT   vsim icount drain target  (default: 5000)
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
27	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
28	
29	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-icount_spin}"
30	QEMU_ICOUNT="${QEMU_ICOUNT:-10000}"
31	VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-5000}"
32	
33	MODE_LABEL="Icount switching (M3)"
34	echo "=========================================================="
35	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
36	echo "Fixture: ${FIXTURE} (CSR-less tight loop)"
37	echo "QEMU1 trigger: plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"
38	echo "vsim  trigger: --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT  (CSR matcher disabled)"
39	echo "=========================================================="
40	
41	hybrid_resolve_paths
42	
43	SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
44	SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-rt.XXXXXX)
45	STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
46	STATE_V=$(mktemp)
47	QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
48	VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
49	trap 'rm -f "$SHM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
50	truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
51	
52	read_u64() { python3 -c "
53	import struct
54	with open('$1', 'rb') as f:
55	    f.seek($2); print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
56	"; }
57	
58	echo "[demo M3 1/2] QEMU1 + plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT -> drain"
59	set +e
60	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
61	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
62	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
63	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
64	  -nographic \
65	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
66	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
67	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT" \
68	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
69	QRC=$?
70	set -e
71	
72	if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
73	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 expected exit 200, got $QRC"
74	  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"; exit 1
75	fi
76	if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
77	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 state empty"; exit 1
78	fi
79	
80	PC_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 16)
81	PC_Q_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_Q")
82	if [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
83	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 drained pc=$PC_Q_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
84	fi
85	echo "  QEMU1 drain: pc=$PC_Q_HEX"
86	
87	echo "[demo M3 2/2] vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT -> drain"
88	set +e
89	timeout 120 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
90	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
91	  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
92	  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
93	  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
94	VRC=$?
95	set -e
96	
97	if [ "$VRC" -ne 200 ]; then
98	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim expected exit 200, got $VRC"
99	  tail -20 "$VSIM_LOG"; exit 1
100	fi
101	if [ ! -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
102	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim state empty"; exit 1
103	fi
104	
105	PC_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 16)
106	PC_V_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_V")
107	if [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
108	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
109	fi
110	echo "  vsim drain: pc=$PC_V_HEX"
111	
112	echo "[demo M3] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): both drains inside icount_spin ROI"

1	"""Subprocess factories for QEMU and vsim.
2	
3	argv builders are pure (no I/O), so they unit-test cleanly. The actual
4	process spawn goes through `subprocess.Popen` directly in `runner.py`.
5	
6	QEMU 2's launch (`qemu_resume_argv`) mirrors `qemu_handback.hpp::spawn_qemu`
7	in the vsim sources. The key invariant is: in shared-mmap (tier-2) mode,
8	`-kernel` is OMITTED so QEMU 2 does not re-zero `.bss` / re-load `.data`
9	on top of phase-2 memory.
10	"""
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	import socket
14	from pathlib import Path
15	
16	
17	def qemu_drain_argv(qemu_bin: Path, elf: Path, plugin: Path,
18	                    mmap_path: Path, mmap_size: int,
19	                    state_out: Path) -> list[str]:
20	    """QEMU 1: load ELF, drain on plugin's CSR/PC trigger, exit 200."""
21	    mem_obj = (
22	        f"memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,"
23	        f"mem-path={mmap_path},size={mmap_size}"
24	    )
25	    return [
26	        str(qemu_bin),
27	        "-object", mem_obj,
28	        "-machine", "andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem",
29	        "-cpu", "andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512",
30	        "-nographic",
31	        "-semihosting-config", "enable=on,target=native",
32	        "-bios", str(elf),
33	        "-plugin", f"{plugin},outfile={state_out}",
34	    ]
35	
36	
37	def vsim_argv(vsim_bin: Path, elf: Path,
38	              state_in: Path, state_out: Path,
39	              mmap_path: Path, mmap_size: int,
40	              shared_mem_base: int = 0x00000000) -> list[str]:
41	    """vsim: resume from QEMU 1 state, run ROI, drain, exit 200.
42	
43	    `--qemu-binary` is intentionally omitted - the orchestrator spawns
44	    QEMU 2 externally, so vsim's in-process handback path stays dormant
45	    (see hybrid_vsim/src/main.cpp:478-490, kept for the bash harness).
46	    """
47	    return [
48	        str(vsim_bin),
49	        str(elf),
50	        "--resume-from-qemu", str(state_in),
51	        "--handoff-out", str(state_out),
52	        "--shared-mem-path", str(mmap_path),
53	        "--shared-mem-size", str(mmap_size),
54	        "--shared-mem-base", hex(shared_mem_base),
55	    ]
56	
57	
58	def qemu_resume_argv(qemu_bin: Path, mmap_path: Path, mmap_size: int,
59	                    gdb_port: int) -> list[str]:
60	    """QEMU 2: start halted under gdbstub. No `-kernel`: shared mmap
61	    already holds the ELF text/data from QEMU 1's launch."""
62	    mem_obj = (
63	        f"memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,"
64	        f"mem-path={mmap_path},size={mmap_size}"
65	    )
66	    return [
67	        str(qemu_bin),
68	        "-object", mem_obj,
69	        "-machine", "andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem",
70	        "-cpu", "andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512",
71	        "-bios", "none",
72	        "-nographic",
73	        "-semihosting-config", "enable=on,target=native",
74	        "-gdb", f"tcp::{gdb_port}",
75	        "-S",
76	    ]
77	
78	
79	def pick_free_port() -> int:
80	    """Ask the kernel for a free loopback port. Subject to a small TOCTOU
81	    race vs. the QEMU bind that follows; in practice safe enough for
82	    test-driver use, mirrors `qemu_handback.hpp::pick_free_port`."""
83	    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
84	        s.bind(("127.0.0.1", 0))
85	        return s.getsockname()[1]

201	
202	    Validates `meta.json` via `validate_replay_meta`, allocates a fresh
203	    /dev/shm mmap sized to `meta.mem_size`, overwrites it with
204	    `snap_dir/mem`, copies `snap_dir/state.bin` into `work_dir` as the

217	    `state_paths` has exactly one entry (the drained state); for qemu,
218	    `state_paths` is empty (`end_of_program` does not produce a state
219	    file).

1	"""Session-plan grammar for the orchestrator.
2	
3	A plan is a YAML document with two top-level keys:
4	
5	    session:
6	      mem_size:  int      # bytes; size of the shared /dev/shm mmap
7	      mmap_path: str|null # null => orchestrator picks under /dev/shm
8	      elf:       str      # ELF launched by every spawn in the session
9	
10	    steps:
11	      - model:   "qemu" | "vsim"
12	        trigger: { kind: ..., <kind-specific params...> }
13	        on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/N" }   # optional
14	
15	Trigger kinds recognised today (the parser accepts any kind; whether a
16	given trigger is actually supported by the targeted model is the spawn
17	layer's concern, landing in F4/F5/F6/F8):
18	
19	    csr             -- CSR/PC matcher (today's default)
20	    icount          -- retire-count threshold (F5/F6)
21	    phase           -- BBV slice consume (F11)
22	    end_of_program  -- spawn runs to workload completion (rc=0)
23	"""
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import dataclasses
27	from pathlib import Path
28	from typing import Any
29	
30	import yaml
31	
32	
33	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
34	class TriggerSpec:
35	    kind: str
36	    params: dict[str, Any] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=dict)
37	
38	
39	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
40	class Step:
41	    model: str
42	    trigger: TriggerSpec
43	    archive_as: str | None = None
44	
45	
46	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
47	class Session:
48	    elf: str
49	    mem_size: int
50	    mmap_path: str | None
51	    steps: tuple[Step, ...]
52	
53	
54	_VALID_MODELS = frozenset({"qemu", "vsim"})
55	
56	
57	def load_plan(path: str | Path) -> Session:
58	    raw = yaml.safe_load(Path(path).read_text())
59	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
60	        raise ValueError(f"plan {path}: top-level must be a mapping")
61	    if "session" not in raw or "steps" not in raw:
62	        raise ValueError(
63	            f"plan {path}: missing 'session' or 'steps' top-level key"
64	        )
65	
66	    session_raw = raw["session"]
67	    steps_raw = raw["steps"]
68	    if not isinstance(steps_raw, list) or not steps_raw:
69	        raise ValueError(f"plan {path}: 'steps' must be a non-empty list")
70	
71	    steps = tuple(_parse_step(s, path, i) for i, s in enumerate(steps_raw))
72	    return Session(
73	        elf=str(session_raw["elf"]),
74	        mem_size=int(session_raw["mem_size"]),
75	        mmap_path=session_raw.get("mmap_path"),
76	        steps=steps,
77	    )
78	
79	
80	def _parse_step(d: dict[str, Any], path: str | Path, idx: int) -> Step:
81	    model = d.get("model")
82	    if model not in _VALID_MODELS:
83	        raise ValueError(
84	            f"plan {path} step {idx}: model={model!r} not in {sorted(_VALID_MODELS)}"
85	        )
86	
87	    trig_raw = d.get("trigger")
88	    if not isinstance(trig_raw, dict) or "kind" not in trig_raw:
89	        raise ValueError(
90	            f"plan {path} step {idx}: 'trigger' must be a mapping with 'kind'"
91	        )
92	    kind = str(trig_raw["kind"])
93	    params = {k: v for k, v in trig_raw.items() if k != "kind"}
94	
95	    on_exit = d.get("on_exit") or {}
96	    archive_as = on_exit.get("archive_as") if isinstance(on_exit, dict) else None
97	
98	    return Step(
99	        model=str(model),
100	        trigger=TriggerSpec(kind=kind, params=params),
101	        archive_as=archive_as,
102	    )

98:    return Step(
100:        trigger=TriggerSpec(kind=kind, params=params),
101:        archive_as=archive_as,

1	# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
2	#
3	# Toolchain location comes from the repo-root config.env (loaded by
4	# `source ./setup.sh`, which exports HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN). Override on the
5	# command line with `make TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain ...` if you want
6	# to bypass the env var.
7	
8	TOOLCHAIN ?= $(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)
9	ifeq ($(strip $(TOOLCHAIN)),)
10	$(error HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN is not set. Run `source ./setup.sh` from the repo root, or pass `TOOLCHAIN=/path/to/toolchain` on the command line)
11	endif
12	CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
13	LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
14	OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump
15	
16	CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
17	LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static
18	
19	# All fixtures share test.ld (load base 0x00100000, andes_ae350 DRAM).
20	RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
21	               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
22	               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival
23	
24	# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
25	# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
26	# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
27	# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
28	# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
29	# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
30	# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
31	# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
32	RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
33	RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
34	# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
35	# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
36	PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr
37	
38	RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
39	RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
40	                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
41	# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
42	# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
43	# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
44	# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
45	RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
46	# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
47	# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
48	# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
49	# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
50	# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
51	RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
52	RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
53	                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
54	# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
55	# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
56	RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
57	                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
58	
59	.PHONY: all clean dump demo
60	
61	all: spin.elf icount_spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
62	     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
63	     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \

65

67	# by EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL handoff_smoke.cpp). They still build for completeness
68	# but are not in active CTest. All targets now share test.ld at 0x00100000.

70	# Switching-mode demo runner: builds the fixtures if needed, then walks
71	# T1..T7 from $(DEMO_DIR), printing a banner per mode and a final
72	# pass/fail tally. Honors FILTER=<regex> to subset the run.
73	demo: all
74		@bash demo.sh
75	
76	spin.elf: spin.S test.ld
77		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
78		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@
79	
80	# CSR-less spin for F5 plugin-icount e2e. Linked at 0x00100000 (test.ld)
81	# so andes_ae350's MROM at 0x80000000 stays clear when loaded via -bios.
82	icount_spin.elf: icount_spin.S test.ld

88		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff.o -o $@
89

91		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
92		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_exit.o -o $@
93	
94	handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
95		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
96		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@
97	
98	$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
99		$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
100		$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@
101	
102	# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
103	# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x00100000) -- ld
104	# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
105	$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
106		$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
107		  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

1	"""Session runner: iterate `session.steps`, dispatch each spawn, validate state.
2	
3	F8 turned the F4 hard-coded 3-spawn shape into a generic loop over
4	`session.steps`. Each step nominates a (model, trigger) pair; the runner
5	dispatches per-(model, trigger.kind) to the right argv builder and exit-code
6	contract:
7	
8	  (qemu, csr|icount|phase)    drain spawn: writes state_out, expects rc=200.
9	  (qemu, end_of_program)      resume spawn: gdbstub-restores state_in, runs to
10	                              workload completion, expects rc=0.
11	  (vsim, csr|icount|phase)    vsim drain: reads state_in, writes state_out,
12	                              expects rc=200.
13	
14	The shared `/dev/shm` mmap is owned by `MmapOwner` for the whole session so
15	the fd survives every spawn boundary (children mmap by path).
16	
17	Archive (`step.archive_as`) is accepted as a no-op in F8; F9 wires the
18	copy-out to `snap/N/`.
19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import contextlib
23	import os
24	import subprocess
25	import sys
26	import tempfile
27	from dataclasses import dataclass
28	from pathlib import Path
29	
30	from orchestrator.abi import HybridStateV1, validate_state_file
31	from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
32	from orchestrator.gdbstub import ChildDiedDuringConnect, restore_and_continue
33	from orchestrator.mmap_owner import MmapOwner
34	from orchestrator.plan import Session, Step
35	from orchestrator.spawn import (
36	    pick_free_port,
37	    qemu_drain_argv,
38	    qemu_resume_argv,
39	    vsim_argv,
40	)
41	
42	# Drain triggers: the spawn exits 200 with a state file on disk.
43	_DRAIN_TRIGGERS = frozenset({"csr", "icount", "phase"})
44	
45	
46	@dataclass(frozen=True)
47	class SessionResult:
48	    """Per-session run outcome.
49	
50	    `exit_code` is 0 on success (every step matched its expected return), or
51	    the last spawn's non-conformant rc on failure (e.g. drain expected but
52	    workload returned 0).
53	
54	    `state_paths` lists the state-file Path produced by each drain step in
55	    spawn order. Resume-and-finish steps (trigger `end_of_program`) do not
56	    contribute a path.
57	    """
58	    exit_code: int
59	    state_paths: tuple[Path, ...]
60	
61	
62	@dataclass(frozen=True)
63	class Artifacts:
64	    qemu: Path
65	    vsim: Path
66	    plugin: Path
67	
68	    @classmethod
69	    def from_env(cls, project_root: Path) -> "Artifacts":
70	        return cls(
71	            qemu=Path(os.environ.get(
72	                "VSIM_QEMU", project_root / "build" / "qemu"
73	                / "qemu-system-riscv64",
74	            )),
75	            vsim=Path(os.environ.get(
76	                "VSIM_BIN", project_root / "hybrid_vsim" / "build"
77	                / "sim_ax45mpv_premium",
78	            )),
79	            plugin=Path(os.environ.get(
80	                "VSIM_PLUGIN", project_root / "qemu_plugin"
81	                / "libhybrid_handoff.so",
82	            )),
83	        )
84	
85	
86	def _wait(proc: subprocess.Popen, timeout: float) -> int:
87	    try:
88	        return proc.wait(timeout=timeout)
89	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
90	        proc.kill()
91	        proc.wait()
92	        raise
93	
94	
95	def run_session(session: Session, project_root: Path,
96	                artifacts: Artifacts | None = None,
97	                spawn_timeout: float = 90.0,
98	                work_dir: Path | None = None,
99	                archive_root: Path | None = None,
100	                initial_state_in: Path | None = None) -> SessionResult:
101	    """Execute every step in `session.steps` and return the aggregate result.
102	
103	    If `work_dir` is provided, state files persist under it as
104	    `orch-state-{idx}.bin`. Otherwise a `TemporaryDirectory` is created and
105	    cleaned up on exit - state paths are only meaningful while the runner
106	    holds them.
107	
108	    `archive_root` is the parent directory under which `step.archive_as`
109	    resolves: a step with `archive_as: "snap/0"` writes its artefacts into
110	    `archive_root / "snap" / "0"`. Defaults to `project_root`. Steps
111	    without `archive_as` skip the archive write.
112	
113	    `initial_state_in` seeds the first step's `state_in` (the input to a
114	    drain or end_of_program spawn). F10 (`replay_checkpoint`) sets this to
115	    the archive's `state.bin` so the first step can resume mid-workload.
116	    Default `None` matches today's behavior: a session starts cold and the
117	    first step must be a model+trigger combination that does not require a
118	    prior state file.
119	    """
120	    if not session.steps:
121	        raise ValueError("session must have at least one step")
122	    if any(s.trigger.params.get("hart_id", 0) != 0 for s in session.steps):
123	        raise ValueError("multi-hart (hart_id != 0) is out of scope for v1")
124	
125	    artifacts = artifacts or Artifacts.from_env(project_root)
126	    elf = project_root / session.elf
127	    archive_root = archive_root or project_root
128	    cpu_variant = _cpu_variant_from_vsim(artifacts.vsim)
129	
130	    with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:
131	        if work_dir is None:
132	            wd = Path(stack.enter_context(
133	                tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="orch-work-")
134	            ))
135	        else:
136	            wd = work_dir
137	
138	        if session.mmap_path:
139	            shm = stack.enter_context(
140	                MmapOwner.attach(session.mmap_path, session.mem_size)
141	            )
142	        else:
143	            shm = stack.enter_context(MmapOwner.allocate(session.mem_size))
144	
145	        state_paths: list[Path] = []
146	        prev_state: Path | None = initial_state_in
147	        last_rc = 0
148	        for idx, step in enumerate(session.steps):
149	            state_out = (
150	                wd / f"orch-state-{idx}.bin"
151	                if step.trigger.kind in _DRAIN_TRIGGERS else None
152	            )
153	            last_rc = _execute_step(
154	                step, artifacts, elf, shm, prev_state, state_out,
155	                spawn_timeout,
156	            )
157	            if state_out is not None:
158	                validate_state_file(state_out)
159	                state_paths.append(state_out)
160	                prev_state = state_out
161	                if step.archive_as is not None:
162	                    write_archive(
163	                        archive_root / step.archive_as,
164	                        state_path=state_out,
165	                        mmap_path=shm.path, mmap_size=shm.size,
166	                        elf_path=session.elf,
167	                        source_step_index=idx,
168	                        cpu_variant=cpu_variant,
169	                        region_id=step.archive_as,
170	                        slice_index=_slice_index_from_archive_as(
171	                            step.archive_as
172	                        ),
173	                    )
174	        return SessionResult(exit_code=last_rc, state_paths=tuple(state_paths))
175	
176	
177	def _cpu_variant_from_vsim(vsim_bin: Path) -> str | None:
178	    """Best-effort: vsim binary name encodes CPU variant (sim_<variant>).
179	
180	    Returns None when the binary name does not match the convention, so
181	    F10's replay validation can tell "archive lacks the field" from
182	    "archive claims a mismatched variant".
183	    """
184	    name = vsim_bin.name
185	    if name.startswith("sim_"):
186	        return name[len("sim_"):]
187	    return None
188	
189	
190	def _slice_index_from_archive_as(archive_as: str) -> int | None:
191	    """Parse the trailing path component as int; None on non-numeric."""
192	    tail = Path(archive_as).name
193	    try:
194	        return int(tail)
195	    except ValueError:
196	        return None
197	
198	
199	def _execute_step(step: Step, arts: Artifacts, elf: Path, shm: MmapOwner,
200	                  state_in: Path | None, state_out: Path | None,
201	                  timeout: float) -> int:
202	    if step.model == "qemu":
203	        if step.trigger.kind in _DRAIN_TRIGGERS:
204	            assert state_out is not None
205	            _qemu_drain(arts, elf, shm, state_out, timeout)
206	            return 0
207	        if step.trigger.kind == "end_of_program":
208	            if state_in is None:
209	                raise ValueError(
210	                    f"qemu/end_of_program step requires prior drain state; "
211	                    f"step has no state_in"
212	                )
213	            return _qemu_resume_to_end(arts, shm, state_in, timeout)
214	        raise ValueError(
215	            f"unsupported (model=qemu, trigger={step.trigger.kind})"
216	        )
217	    if step.model == "vsim":
218	        if step.trigger.kind in _DRAIN_TRIGGERS:
219	            if state_in is None:
220	                raise ValueError(
221	                    f"vsim step requires state_in from a prior drain"
222	                )
223	            assert state_out is not None
224	            _vsim_drain(arts, elf, state_in, state_out, shm, timeout)
225	            return 0
226	        raise ValueError(
227	            f"unsupported (model=vsim, trigger={step.trigger.kind})"
228	        )
229	    raise ValueError(f"unknown model: {step.model!r}")
230	
231	
232	def _qemu_drain(arts: Artifacts, elf: Path, shm: MmapOwner,
233	                state_out: Path, timeout: float) -> None:
234	    argv = qemu_drain_argv(arts.qemu, elf, arts.plugin, shm.path, shm.size,
235	                           state_out)
236	    proc = subprocess.Popen(argv, stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
237	                            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
238	                            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
239	    rc = _wait(proc, timeout)
240	    if rc != 200:
241	        raise RuntimeError(
242	            f"qemu drain expected exit 200, got {rc}. argv={argv}"
243	        )
244	
245	
246	def _vsim_drain(arts: Artifacts, elf: Path, state_in: Path, state_out: Path,
247	                shm: MmapOwner, timeout: float) -> None:
248	    argv = vsim_argv(arts.vsim, elf, state_in, state_out, shm.path, shm.size)
249	    proc = subprocess.Popen(argv, stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
250	                            stdout=sys.stderr, stderr=sys.stderr)

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T4: BBV-emit switching mode (M4) + SimPoint clustering integration.
3	#
4	# QEMU + plugin runs icount_spin.elf with bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M.
5	# Plugin writes one SimPoint .bb row per N retired insns; expected
6	# rows = M/N. Then tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint clusters the .bb file
7	# and produces simpts (slice -> cluster) + weights (cluster -> mass).
8	#
9	# Outputs are persisted under $WORKDIR (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)
10	# so T5/T7 can consume them. WORKDIR is printed on PASS.
11	#
12	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
13	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
14	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
15	#   VSIM_ELF      a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
16	#   SIMPOINT_BIN  the built simpoint clustering binary
17	#   WORKDIR       persistent dir for bbv/simpts/weights (default: mktemp -d)
18	#   SLICE         insns per BBV row (default: 1000)
19	#   ICOUNT        total insns before drain (default: 10000)
20	#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2; tiny BBVs need conservative K)
21	
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
25	. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"
26	
27	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-icount_spin}"
28	hybrid_resolve_paths
29	SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
30	WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-bbv-XXXXXX)}"
31	SLICE="${SLICE:-1000}"
32	ICOUNT="${ICOUNT:-10000}"
33	MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
34	
35	MODE_LABEL="BBV emit + SimPoint cluster (M4)"
36	echo "=========================================================="
37	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
38	echo "Workload: ${FIXTURE} (tight 2-insn loop, CSR-less)"
39	echo "Plugin: bbv_out=...,slice=$SLICE,icount=$ICOUNT"
40	echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
41	echo "=========================================================="
42	
43	if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
44	  echo "[demo M4] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"
45	  exit 77
46	fi
47	
48	mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
49	BBV_OUT="$WORKDIR/icount_spin.bb"
50	SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
51	WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
52	STATE_OUT="$WORKDIR/state_qemu.bin"
53	QEMU_LOG="$WORKDIR/qemu.log"
54	SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
55	
56	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
57	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-bbv-shared.XXXXXX)
58	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM"' EXIT
59	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
60	
61	EXPECTED_ROWS=$((ICOUNT / SLICE))
62	
63	echo "[demo M4 1/3] QEMU + plugin: emit BBV ($EXPECTED_ROWS rows expected)"
64	set +e
65	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
66	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
67	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
68	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
69	  -nographic \
70	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
71	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
72	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,icount=$ICOUNT,bbv_out=$BBV_OUT,slice=$SLICE" \
73	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
74	RC=$?
75	set -e
76	
77	if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
78	  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: QEMU expected exit 200, got $RC"
79	  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
80	  exit 1
81	fi
82
